signals.title: Policy
signals.summary: Public sector bodies are establishing international agreements and national frameworks to address emerging technology challenges, including online safety, subsea cable security, and artificial intelligence risks.

what_to_notice: International agreements are being formed, such as a UK-Australia pact on AI security risks and a UK-France science and technology deal.
Academic research highlights that large technology firms are using lobbying tactics similar to those historically used by tobacco companies.
National security concerns are prompting plans to strengthen protections for subsea internet cables.

signals.title: Transformation
signals.summary: Public organisations are testing new delivery models and technologies, including generative AI agents and collaborative service teams, to reform public services and improve user experiences.

what_to_notice: The Department for Education is experimenting with generative AI agents to assist delivery officers, highlighting where they succeed and fail.
The DVLA is partnering with CustomerFirst to form a joint team aimed at improving the Drivers Medical service.
The Scottish Government has established a dedicated cabinet role to oversee public sector reform.

signals.title: Procurement
signals.summary: Public sector buyers are planning large-scale technology frameworks and exploring open-source alternatives to shape how digital services are sourced and secured.

what_to_notice: The NHS is preparing a second version of its £2 billion digital health framework for electronic patient records and other systems.
Manchester NHS is seeking a supplier for a £600,000 "digital front door" platform to improve ADHD services.
The Government Digital Service is exploring open-source options to help build a common technology stack for local authorities.

signals.title: AI and automation
signals.summary: Research and practical testing are helping organisations understand the risks, lobbying practices, and operational limits of artificial intelligence in public administration.

what_to_notice: The US Government Accountability Office has developed an AI competitiveness framework to help policymakers measure national AI strength.
A study warns that large technology firms are adopting lobbying and communication playbooks similar to those used by tobacco companies.
Practical testing at the Department for Education shows where generative AI agents improve delivery and where human oversight remains necessary.

signals.title: Local government
signals.summary: Local authorities are exploring collaborative models, open-source software, and mission-led approaches to address financial pressures and improve community outcomes.

links.title: Stay the course on a Total Place race

what_to_notice: GDS Local is evaluating open-source products to help councils build a shared, common technology stack.
Luton Council is focusing on skills, graduate opportunities, and potential plans for a mayoral authority.
Discussions are focusing on "Total Place" and neighbourhood-based models to bring healthcare closer to local communities.

signals.title: Accessibility
signals.summary: Public and civic organisations are applying design standards to physical and digital services, ensuring they are accessible and functional for diverse user groups.

Making Games

what_to_notice: Defra has launched a public beta for digital waste tracking, allowing permitted waste sites to register and submit data digitally.
The Center for Civic Design has published guidance on designing mail ballot envelopes that work reliably for both voters and the postal service.
A joint team from DVLA and CustomerFirst is working to improve the accessibility and decision-making of the Drivers Medical service.
